key_insight: The paper derives a computable a posteriori error estimator for neural approximations of fully coupled FBSDEs that explicitly accounts for mismatch between the forward auxiliary control and the backward neural output.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a diagnostic tool to assess reliability, tune training, and improve reproducibility of deep-learning solvers for stochastic control and PDE-related systems.
method: Prove a continuous-time stability estimate under perturbations of drift, diffusion, generator, terminal data, and auxiliary control.
Transfer the stability bound to discrete time to obtain computable error indicators.
Use terminal defect, pathwise residual, and control mismatch as the three main a posteriori terms; mismatch vanishes when the auxiliary control equals the backward approximation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on a linear-quadratic FBSDE with a known solution and a multidimensional Burgers-type FBSDE without a reference solution to demonstrate the indicators and mismatch penalty.
watch_item: The bound depends on accurately computing residuals and the auxiliary-control mismatch, so implementation details and discretization quality can strongly affect the diagnostic value.
paper_title: A Posteriori Error Analysis for Decoupled Neural Approximations of Fully Coupled FBSDEs with Control Mismatch
